title: calawaya nanegalensis
intro: Calawaya nanegalensis, also known as the "Andean Mint", is a type of flowering plant native to the Andes mountains in Ecuador. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Calawaya nanegalensis include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and are often used to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and promote relaxation.
